abstract: The length of encoded Base32 or Base64 lines.
overview:
## Discussion

Some systems can’t handle excessively long lines, or may be defined to limit lines to specific lengths (for example RFC1421 - 64, and RFC2045 - 76).

The corresponding value may be set to any positive value using a [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFNumber] to limit to a specific length (values smaller then X for Base32 or Y for Base64 are assume to be X or Y), or to zero for no specific limit. Either of the string constants [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecLineLength64] (RFC1421), or [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecLineLength76] (RFC2045) may be used to set line lengths of 64 or 76 bytes.

# kSecEncodeLineLengthAttribute

**Global Variable**

The length of encoded Base32 or Base64 lines.

## Declaration

```swift
let kSecEncodeLineLengthAttribute: CFString
```

## Discussion

Some systems can’t handle excessively long lines, or may be defined to limit lines to specific lengths (for example RFC1421 - 64, and RFC2045 - 76).

The corresponding value may be set to any positive value using a [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFNumber] to limit to a specific length (values smaller then X for Base32 or Y for Base64 are assume to be X or Y), or to zero for no specific limit. Either of the string constants [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecLineLength64] (RFC1421), or [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecLineLength76] (RFC2045) may be used to set line lengths of 64 or 76 bytes.
title: kSecEncodeLineLengthAttribute
